phishing.description: Fraudulent attempts to obtain sensitive information by disguising as a trustworthy entity
examples: Someone claiming to be from the bank asking for your PIN
Email saying your account will be closed unless you click a link
SMS asking you to verify your BVN by replying with details
WhatsApp message offering free money if you share your account number
responses: ⚠️ This looks like a phishing attempt. Never share your PIN, password, or OTP with anyone.
🚨 Warning: Legitimate banks never ask for your PIN via message or call.
🛡️ Security Alert: This request appears suspicious. Always verify through official channels.

social_engineering.description: Manipulation tactics to trick users into revealing confidential information
examples: Caller claiming to be bank manager needing urgent verification
Message saying you won a lottery but need to pay processing fee
Someone pretending to be IT support asking for login credentials
responses: ⚠️ This appears to be a social engineering attempt. Verify the identity through official channels.
🚨 Never make urgent financial decisions based on unexpected calls or messages.
🛡️ SznPay will never ask you to transfer money to verify your account.

account_takeover.description: Unauthorized access to user accounts through stolen credentials
indicators: password reset requests not initiated by user
examples: Login attempt from a new device in a different country
Sudden change of email or phone number
responses: 🚨 Suspicious activity detected. Your account has been temporarily locked.
⚠️ We noticed a login attempt from an unusual location. Was this you?
🛡️ For your security, please verify this activity.

examples: Transaction at 3 AM when user normally transacts during day
responses: ⚠️ This transaction appears unusual. Please confirm it's legitimate.
🚨 Fraud alert: This transaction pattern is suspicious.
🛡️ For your protection, we need additional verification.

malware.description: Malicious software designed to damage or gain unauthorized access
examples: Banking app asking to install from unknown source
responses: 🚨 Potential malware detected. Only download SznPay from official app stores.
⚠️ Your device may be compromised. Please scan for malware.
🛡️ For security, we don't support rooted or jailbroken devices.

sim_swap.description: Fraudsters transfer victim's phone number to a new SIM card
indicators: OTP requests not initiated by user
examples: OTP codes being received but user didn't request them
responses: 🚨 Critical: Possible SIM swap attack detected. Contact us immediately.
⚠️ Your phone number may have been compromised. Verify with your network provider.
🛡️ All transactions have been suspended for your protection.

rules: Mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
No common words or personal information
tips: Don't use the same password across sites

rules: Always verify recipient details before sending money
Install apps only from official stores
Use device lock (PIN, fingerprint, face ID)
Don't root or jailbreak your device

tips: SznPay will never ask for your PIN
We'll never ask you to transfer money to verify your account
When in doubt, contact us directly

common_scams.name: Fake Bank Alert
common_scams.description: Fraudsters send fake credit alerts to trick merchants
common_scams.how_it_works: Scammer shows fake SMS or email claiming payment was made
common_scams.prevention: Always verify payments in your actual bank app, not SMS
common_scams.example: Someone buys goods and shows a fake ₦50,000 credit alert

common_scams.name: Romance Scam
common_scams.description: Fraudster builds romantic relationship then asks for money
common_scams.how_it_works: Create fake profile, build trust over time, then request financial help
common_scams.prevention: Never send money to someone you haven't met in person
common_scams.example: Online partner asks for money for emergency medical bills

common_scams.name: Investment Scam
common_scams.how_it_works: Promise high returns with little risk, use new investors to pay old ones
common_scams.prevention: If it sounds too good to be true, it probably is
common_scams.example: Platform promising 50% monthly returns on investments

common_scams.name: Fake Customer Support
common_scams.how_it_works: Call or message claiming to be from bank, ask for sensitive info
common_scams.prevention: Never share credentials with anyone claiming to be support
common_scams.example: Caller says your account is compromised and needs your PIN to fix it

common_scams.name: Advance Fee Fraud (419)
common_scams.description: Request upfront payment for promised larger sum
common_scams.how_it_works: Claim you won lottery/inheritance but need to pay fees first
common_scams.prevention: Legitimate winnings never require upfront payment
common_scams.example: Email saying you won $1M but need to pay ₦50,000 processing fee

red_flags: Offers that seem too good to be true
Requests to move conversation off official channels
safe_banking_habits: Don't share account access with others

suspicious_indicators: Structuring (breaking large transactions into smaller ones)

low.description: Minor security event with no immediate risk

critical.response: Freeze account, immediate investigation, law enforcement

kpis.description: Percentage of fraudulent transactions detected before completion
Percentage of legitimate transactions incorrectly flagged as fraud
Average time to detect security incidents
Average time to respond to security incidents
Percentage of account takeover attempts prevented
Percentage of users who can identify common scams

metadata.description: Comprehensive security knowledge base for AI-powered threat detection and user education
